principles: Select libraries from the user's drawing intent, not from a fixed template.
Use a library when it provides reusable visual language or recognizable components for the scene.
Prefer sparse, purposeful library use over decorative insertion.
Keep generated scenes editable; library components are building blocks, not flattened images.
Record selected library ids in appState.codex.libraries so later read-back can explain why they were used.

libraries.name: Basic UX/wireframing elements
libraries.author: gabi.macakova
libraries.description: Basic UX and wireframing controls such as buttons, text boxes, text areas, checkboxes, drop-down menus, toggles, image placeholders, and profile photos.
useWhen: The user asks for low-fidelity product screens, UI layout, app onboarding screens, page variants, or reusable interface controls.
Codex needs familiar UI primitives instead of drawing raw rectangles from scratch.
avoidWhen: The scene is purely conceptual, architectural, or data-focused with no UI surface.

libraries.name: Emojis
libraries.author: Anumitha Apollo
libraries.description: A collection of hand-drawn emoji elements.
useWhen: The user asks for playful product sketches, emotional states, onboarding, learning apps, gamified flows, or lightweight visual markers.
A small icon-like accent can make a user journey or product board easier to scan.
avoidWhen: The diagram is formal architecture, compliance, finance, or a serious implementation plan.

libraries.name: Decision flow control
libraries.author: James Wiens
useWhen: The user asks for decision trees, approval flows, conditional logic, yes/no branching, process gates, or flowcharts with explicit choices.
A flow would be clearer with recognized decision-control shapes.
avoidWhen: The flow is linear and has no branching condition.

libraries.name: Business Model Templates
libraries.author: Stephan Hellerbrand
libraries.description: Business Model Canvas and Value Proposition Canvas templates for modeling business aspects.
useWhen: The user asks to explore a product/business idea, business model canvas, value proposition, market strategy, revenue model, or customer segment map.
The output should be a structured business canvas rather than a generic product board.
avoidWhen: The request is only about UI layout, code architecture, or a narrow implementation flow.

libraries.name: Data Viz
libraries.author: dbs-sticky
libraries.description: Common chart components used in data visualizations.
useWhen: The user asks for dashboards, metric explanations, data products, reports, analytics flows, or chart-like visual summaries.
The diagram needs recognizable chart components rather than plain data boxes.
avoidWhen: The diagram has no quantitative or analytical surface.
